exercises.title: Exercise 1 (6 points)
exercises.introduction_context: The following table 1 shows the sequence of semaphore operations at the beginning and at the end of the tasks A, B, C.

semaphore_init_table.description: Table 2 : Initial values of the semaphore variables

Question.question_text: Determine for the 4 cases a, b, c, d, given in the table 1.1, whether and in which sequence the tasks are executed, using the initializations of the semaphore variables given in table 2.

exercises.title: Exercise 2 (8 points)
exercises.introduction_context: A museum allows visitors to enter through the east entrance and leave through the west exit. Arrivals and departures are signaled to the museum controller by the turnstiles at the entrance and exit. At opening time, the museum director signals the controller that the museum is open and then the controller permits both arrivals and departures. At closing time, the director signal that the museum is closed, at which point only departures are permitted by the controller. In this exercise there are four processes/monitor: EAST, WEST, CONTROL and DIRECTOR.

Question.question_text: Identify which of these should be processus and which one a monitor. Justify your response.
Provide the implementation of the monitor in pseudo code, by Implementing the following functions: Function requestEntry, function requestExit.

exercises.title: Exercise 3 (6 points)
exercises.introduction_context: Consider a system with 16 memory locations (common resources) shared by 3 processes (tasks).

banker_table.note: Each process has a maximum demand of 10 memory locations.

Question.question_text: For times t1, t2 and t3, say whether or not there is deadlock. Show that that the state of the system is healthy or not. Assume that each process has a maximum demand of 10 memory locations.
For times t1, t2 and t3, say whether or not there is a risk of deadlock. We show that the state of the system is reliable or not.
